files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Adding process_priority param to Editor.__init__ (_termui_impl.py) and to edit_file() where subprocess.Popen is called, plus threading process_priority through click.edit() signature in termui.py

type: SURVEY
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Adding multi-file support to click.edit(): renaming Editor.edit_file(filename) to Editor.edit_files(filenames) supporting str|Iterable[str], and updating termui.edit() filename param to accept str|Iterable[str]|None with typing overloads. Touches Editor class in _termui_impl.py and edit() function in termui.py.

type: DRAFT
files: src/click/termui.py
region: edit() function (signature, overloads, docstring, body)
code: def edit(
    text: t.AnyStr | None = None,
    editor: str | None = None,
    env: cabc.Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
    require_save: bool = True,
    extension: str = ".txt",
    filename: str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None = None,
    process_priority: str | None = None,
) -> t.AnyStr | None:
    r"""Edits the given text in the defined editor.  If an editor is given
    (should be the full path to the executable but the regular operating
    system search path is used for finding the executable) it overrides
    the detected editor.  Optionally, some environment variables can be
    used.  If the editor is closed without changes, `None` is returned.  In
    case a file is edited directly the return value is always `None` and
    `require_save` and `extension` are ignored.

    If the editor cannot be opened a :exc:`UsageError` is raised.

    Note for Windows: to simplify cross-platform usage, the newlines are
    automatically converted from POSIX to Windows and vice versa.  As such,
    the message here will have ``\n`` as newline markers.

    :param text: the text to edit.
    :param editor: optionally the editor to use.  Defaults to automatic
                   detection.
    :param env: environment variables to forward to the editor.
    :param require_save: if this is true, then not saving in the editor
                         will make the return value become `None`.
    :param extension: the extension to tell the editor about.  This defaults
                      to `.txt` but changing this might change syntax
                      highlighting.
    :param filename: if provided it will edit this file instead of the
                     provided text contents.  It will not use a temporary
                     file as an indirection in that case.  Can be a single
                     filename or an iterable of filenames to edit multiple
                     files at once, if the editor supports it.
    :param process_priority: optionally set the priority the editor process
                             is started with. One of ``"low"``,
                             ``"below_normal"``, ``"normal"``,
                             ``"above_normal"``, or ``"high"``. Defaults to
                             not changing the priority.

    .. versionchanged:: 8.2.0
        ``filename`` can be an iterable of filenames to edit multiple files
        at once.

    .. versionchanged:: 8.2.0
        Added the ``process_priority`` parameter.
    """
    from ._termui_impl import Editor

    ed = Editor(
        editor=editor,
        env=env,
        require_save=require_save,
        extension=extension,
        process_priority=process_priority,
    )

    if filename is None:
        return ed.edit(text)

    if isinstance(filename, str):
        filenames = (filename,)
    else:
        filenames = tuple(filename)

    ed.edit_files(filenames)
    return None
note: Threaded process_priority through alongside filename: str|Iterable[str]|None. Kept it a single non-overloaded def rather than adding @t.overload pairs since return type stays t.AnyStr|None regardless of which branch runs (matches existing style, no behavior change to callers). Let me know if you specifically need overloads for type-checking purposes and I will add them to this same draft.

type: AGREE

note: Confirmed agreed, byte-identical. Also flagging: Editor.edit() (tempfile-based, line ~562 in current source) calls self.edit_file(name) - I will update that single call site to self.edit_files((name,)) since it is solely a consequence of my rename and does not touch your process_priority code. Moving to implementation now.
